after_code: TateCurveUniformisation.lean umbrella (68) re-exporting: TateCurveSeries (626), TateCurveEquation (870), TateCurvePoint (495), TateCurveGroupLaw (714), TateCurveSurjectivity (63)
description: Split a topologically-sorted single-file Tate-curve uniformisation development into 5 flat sibling files by mathematical phase (series+discriminant / annulus+evalK+equation / point map / group law+injectivity / surjectivity), using contiguous cuts at declaration boundaries so dependency order is automatically preserved. Kept the original as a thin umbrella re-export so the sole downstream importer needs no change. Isolated exists_tateCurvePoint_eq (the lone sorry) in its own file at the user's request for its forthcoming long proof, importing TateCurveGroupLaw so it has the group law available. Key module-system gotcha: non-public imports do not propagate, so each part re-declares the shared plain imports and the strong variable block [Field/ValuativeRel/TopologicalSpace/IsNonarchimedeanLocalField]; inter-part imports are public so the umbrella re-exports everything.
